# BMAD Planner Orchestrator

Reads epics and produces an ordered sprint plan that sequences stories for implementation.

## What to do

1. Read `planning-artifacts/epics.md` (all epics and stories)
2. Read all existing story files in `planning-artifacts/stories/` to detect already-created stories
3. Create or update `planning-artifacts/sprint-status.yaml`

## sprint-status.yaml format

```yaml
# Sprint Status — auto-generated by BMAD Planner
last_updated: <date>

development_status:
  epic-1: not-started          # epic-1-setup
  1-1-<story-slug>: ready-for-dev
  1-2-<story-slug>: not-started
  epic-1-retrospective: not-started

  epic-2: not-started
  2-1-<story-slug>: not-started
  epic-2-retrospective: not-started
```

## Status values

| Value | Meaning |
|---|---|
| `not-started` | Story not yet created or started |
| `ready-for-dev` | Story file exists, ready for Amelia |
| `in-progress` | Amelia is currently implementing |
| `review` | Implementation done, awaiting code review |
| `done` | Approved and merged |

## Validation

After generating, confirm:
- Every epic in `epics.md` appears in the plan
- Every story in `epics.md` appears in the plan
- Stories are ordered within each epic (1-1, 1-2, 1-3...)
- Each epic ends with a retrospective entry

## How to invoke

```
run sprint planning from planning-artifacts/epics.md
```
